summary: SG-1: createBinder passes scope as mission at all 3 call sites
detail: All 3 call sites in secretary.ts pass scope as the 4th param: createBinder(id, campaignId, scope, scope). The Interface signature is (laneId, campaignId, mission, scope) — mission is always identical to scope. The missionObjective field on Binder is thus always a copy of laneScope.

summary: SG-2: ensureBinder at binder.ts:436 has zero external callers — dead API
detail: ensureBinder is defined with idempotent get-or-create semantics but never called from secretary.ts or any other module. The secretary always uses createBinder instead, which creates a new binder event in EventStore every time rather than reusing an existing one.

summary: SG-3: Binder shadow state (secretary LaneState.binder) can go stale after addRoleEvidence
detail: LaneState.binder is set during processEvent terminal block but NOT updated by addRoleEvidence. addRoleEvidence calls binderService.addEvidence without refreshing the in-memory lane.binder shadow. Subsequent getLaneBinder calls read the non-null but stale shadow — the EventStore fallback is never reached because the shadow is non-null.
